---
title: "SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures"
format:
  html:
    number-sections: false
---

{{< include _includes/sprfmo-banner.qmd >}}

<div class="paper-title">Candidate Management Procedures: Specifications, Evidence, and Application</div>

::: {.status-note}
This site distinguishes **test candidates**, **shortlisted candidates**, and
**adopted management procedures**. Inclusion in the registry does not imply
endorsement or adoption.
:::

## Purpose

This site is the durable specification and implementation guide for candidate
management procedures (CMPs) evaluated for South Pacific jack mackerel. It is
derived primarily from the current `jmMSE` repository and brings together:

1. the MP29 and MP32 candidate definitions advanced into robustness testing;
2. the end-to-end MSE workflow;
3. evidence from tuning, trade-off, and robustness comparisons; and
4. instructions for applying a selected procedure when new annual data become
   available.

SCW17 Papers 03, 04, 08, and 09 provide historical context and documentation
structure, but they do not define the current candidate registry.

The scientific simulation engine remains in
[`SPRFMO/jmMSE`](https://github.com/SPRFMO/jmMSE). This repository records what
a CMP is, why it remains under consideration, how it is applied, and which
version of code and evidence supports it.

## Start here

- [CMP registry](cmp/index.qmd): the two candidates currently advanced by
  `jmMSE`, MP29 and MP32.
- [MP29](cmp/mp29.qmd) and [MP32](cmp/mp32.qmd): candidate-specific rules and
  annual calculation details.
- [Annual application](application/annual-workflow.qmd): controlled operational
  sequence from new data to advice.
- [Slick explorer](application/slick.qmd): validated conversion of MP29/MP32
  performance outputs for interactive review.
- [MSE workflow](workflow/mse-flow.qmd): where CMPs fit within the assessment
  and simulation system.
- [Evidence and history](evidence/scw17-evidence.qmd): current `jmMSE` evidence
  with SCW17 development context.
- [Traceability](governance/traceability.qmd): source commits, review state, and
  change control.

## Governing principle

The normative CMP specification is separate from explanatory text and MSE
results. A scientific result may change the status of a candidate, but it does
not silently change the estimator, HCR, implementation system, parameter
values, or exceptional-circumstances rules.
